post.summary: POST /v1/agents/import - Import agent from file or built-in example
post.description:
Two modes:
1. **From example** — `POST /v1/agents/import?from-example={name}` (body ignored)
2. **From file** — `POST /v1/agents/import` with a text body in Markdown/YAML/JSON

File mode accepts:
- Markdown with YAML front matter (if starts with ---)
- Pure YAML
- Pure JSON
- Plain text (treated as system prompt, name auto-generated)

If the file contains an `id` field and an agent with that ID already exists,
the agent is updated (upsert). Returns 201 on create, 200 on update.

parameters.name: from-example
parameters.description:
Import from a built-in example by name (e.g. `dad-jokes-agent`).
When set, the request body is ignored.

400.description: Invalid format or input exceeds limits

post.summary: POST /v1/agents/preview - Preview the final agent shape with capabilities applied
post.description:
Returns the merged system prompt and all tools that would be available to the agent.
This is useful for previewing what the agent will look like before saving.

get.summary:
`GET /v1/apps/{app_id}/fcp` — handshake. Always returns the same
generic 404 body for unknown apps so the endpoint cannot be used to
probe which app ids are real.

200.description: Markdown handshake describing the FCP endpoint and how to authenticate. Always `text/markdown`.

404.description: No FCP endpoint at this URL. Single sanitized body covers unknown apps, unpublished apps, apps without an FCP channel, and disabled FCP channels — operator state is never disclosed.

429.description: Per-app FCP rate limit exceeded. `Retry-After: 60` header is set.

requestBody.description: Plain UTF-8 text, or `application/json` of shape `{"message": "..."}`. Maximum 256 KiB.

200.description: Agent reply as Markdown. The `Set-Cookie: fcp_session=...` header is emitted so subsequent POSTs can resume the same conversation.

400.description: Malformed body. Distinct messages for empty body, non-UTF-8 bytes, and JSON that did not parse into the expected shape — each Markdown body points back at the handshake.

401.description: Token required but missing or wrong. Markdown body explains both accepted headers (`Authorization: Bearer` and `X-Everruns-FCP-Token`) and points at the handshake.

404.description: Same generic 404 as the handshake — operator state is never disclosed.

410.description: FCP session expired. Body tells the client to drop the `fcp_session` cookie and POST again.

429.description: Per-app FCP rate limit exceeded. `Retry-After: 60` header is set.

504.description: Agent did not reply within the configured timeout. The same `fcp_session` cookie is set so the client can retry the same conversation.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window to include, such as 24h, 60m, or 7d.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: Optional grouping. Currently only `hour` is supported.

get.description:
Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection for real-time durable system monitoring.

## Connection Lifecycle Events

- **connected**: Sent immediately when the stream is established.
- **snapshot**: Sent when system state changes (health, workers, workflows, tasks, DLQ, circuit breakers).
- **disconnecting**: Sent before the server closes the connection for graceful cycling.
  Data: `{"reason":"connection_cycle","retry_ms":1000}`

## Connection Cycling

Connections are automatically cycled every 10 minutes. Before closing, the server sends
a `disconnecting` event so clients can reconnect seamlessly.

## Retry Hints

Each SSE event includes a `retry:` field (in milliseconds) that hints reconnection timing:
- During active updates: 1000ms
- During idle periods: increases with backoff up to 20000ms
- After `disconnecting` event: 1000ms

200.description: SSE event stream with 'connected', 'snapshot', and 'disconnecting' events

get.description:
Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection for real-time workflow monitoring.

## Connection Lifecycle Events

- **connected**: Sent immediately when the stream is established.
- **snapshot**: Sent when workflow state or events change.
- **disconnecting**: Sent before the server closes the connection for graceful cycling.
  Data: `{"reason":"connection_cycle","retry_ms":1000}`

## Connection Cycling

Connections are automatically cycled every 10 minutes. Before closing, the server sends
a `disconnecting` event so clients can reconnect seamlessly.

## Retry Hints

Each SSE event includes a `retry:` field (in milliseconds) that hints reconnection timing.

200.description: SSE event stream with 'connected', 'snapshot', and 'disconnecting' events

post.description:
Cancels the currently running turn in the session. If no turn is running,
this is a no-op and returns success (idempotent). When a turn is active:
1. Cancel the underlying workflow execution
2. Emit a turn.cancelled event
3. Insert an agent message indicating the turn was cancelled
4. Set the session status back to idle

200.description: Turn cancelled, or no-op (status: no_op) if no turn was running

get.description:
Returns events for a session as a JSON array. Supports filtering by event type
via `types` (positive: only these types) and `exclude` (negative: remove these types).
When both are provided, `types` narrows first, then `exclude` removes from that set.
Both accept only known event types (max 25 per parameter). Unknown types return 400.

## Pagination

Supports backward pagination via `limit` and `before_sequence`:
- `limit=200` returns the last 200 events (oldest→newest)
- `limit=200&before_sequence=4500` returns 200 events before sequence 4500
- When `limit` is provided, the response includes an `X-Total-Count` header
  with the count of non-delta events for the session
- Turn boundary snapping: when fetching older pages, the batch boundary
  snaps to the nearest `turn.started` event to avoid splitting turns
- Without `limit`, all events are returned (backward compatible)

parameters.name: since_id
parameters.description: Filter events with ID greater than this event ID (prefixed format: event_{32-hex})

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: Positive type filter (repeat key). Empty = all types.

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description: Event types to exclude (repeat key); applied after `types`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Max events to return (backward pagination). When set, returns the last N events
(or last N before `before_sequence`). Results are ordered oldest→newest.

parameters.name: before_sequence
parameters.description: Cursor for backward pagination: only return events with sequence < this value.

parameters.name: after_sequence
parameters.description: Forward cursor: only return events with sequence > this value.

parameters.name: around
parameters.description:
Anchor event id: returns up to `window` events on each side (default 50, max 500).
Mutually exclusive with `since_id`, `after_sequence`, and `before_sequence` — 400 if combined.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Window size for `around` (events on each side). Defaults to 50, max 500.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Tag any-match against `events.tags` (repeat key for multiple values).

parameters.name: tool_name
parameters.description: Filter by `data.tool_name` — useful for narrowing `tool.*` events.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Full-text search (Postgres tsvector; substring fallback for in-memory mode).

parameters.name: order_desc
parameters.description: When true, return newest first; default oldest first.

X-Total-Count.description: Total non-delta event count for the session (only present when limit is used)

400.description: Invalid session ID or invalid event type filter

200.description: Per-type counts and time span for the session

get.description:
Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection for real-time event streaming.

## Connection Lifecycle Events

- **connected**: Sent immediately when the stream is established.
  Data: `{"status":"connected"}`

- **disconnecting**: Sent before the server closes the connection for graceful cycling.
  Data: `{"reason":"connection_cycle","retry_ms":100}`
  Clients should reconnect immediately using the `since_id` of the last received event.

## Connection Cycling

Connections are automatically cycled every 5 minutes to prevent stale connections
through proxies and load balancers. Before closing, the server sends a `disconnecting`
event so clients can reconnect seamlessly without missing events.

## Retry Hints

Each SSE event includes a `retry:` field (in milliseconds) that hints how long
clients should wait before reconnecting if the connection is lost:
- During active streaming: 100ms (fast reconnect)
- During idle periods: increases with backoff up to 500ms
- After `disconnecting` event: 100ms (immediate reconnect)

## Resuming Streams

Use the `since_id` query parameter to resume from a specific event. The server
resolves the event ID to its sequence number and returns all subsequent events
ordered by sequence, ensuring reliable ordering even under concurrent writes.

## Event Type Filtering

Use `types` for positive filtering (only return these types) and `exclude` for
negative filtering (remove these types). When both are provided, `types` narrows
first, then `exclude` removes from that set. Both accept only known event types
(max 25 per parameter). Unknown types return 400.

parameters.name: since_id
parameters.description: Filter events with ID greater than this event ID (prefixed format: event_{32-hex})

parameters.name: types
parameters.description:
Positive type filter: only return events matching these types (can be specified multiple times).
When empty, all types are returned. Example: ?types=turn.started&types=turn.completed

parameters.name: exclude
parameters.description:
Event types to exclude from the response (can be specified multiple times).
Applied after `types` filter. Common delta events to exclude: output.message.delta, reason.thinking.delta

200.description:
Server-Sent Events stream. Each SSE message has the wire shape `event: <type>
id: <event_id>
data: <json>

`, where `<type>` is one of the event types in the `EventData` catalog (`turn.started`, `tool.completed`, `reason.thinking.delta`, …), `<event_id>` is the event cursor (`event_{32-hex}` format, usable as `since_id` on reconnect), and `<json>` is the body schema below — a serialized `Event` whose `data` field carries the event-type-specific payload defined in `EventData`. Lifecycle framing events (`connected`, `disconnecting`) use the same SSE shape but carry a minimal JSON object in `data` rather than a full `Event`. See `specs/api-streaming.md` for the SSE convention.
List of secrets (names only, no values)

put.description:
Encrypts and stores multiple secrets in a single request.
Existing secrets with the same name are overwritten.

400.description: Bad request (encryption not configured or invalid input)

AllowedAction.description:
Agent-actionable link describing a follow-up the caller can take. Used in
two contexts:

* **Error recovery** — `ErrorResponse.allowed_actions` carries `rel`s like
  `retry`, `retry-later`, `unarchive`, `get-existing` so the agent knows
  the right next call after a 4xx/429.
* **Entity hypermedia** — `WithUrls<T>.allowed_actions` carries state-aware
  `rel`s like `cancel`, `events`, `self`, `update` on the entity itself
  so the agent can follow links instead of reconstructing routes from
  prose.

The shape is intentionally identical across both contexts; the closed
`rel` vocabulary documented in `specs/api-conventions.md` distinguishes
them.

hint.description:
Short, agent-readable hint (e.g. "Shorten 'name' to <= 200 chars.",
"Cancel the active turn for this session.").

href.description:
Absolute (preferred) or relative URL the caller may invoke
directly. **Always present on entity hypermedia actions**
(`WithUrls<T>.allowed_actions`); **optional on error-recovery
actions** (`ErrorResponse.allowed_actions`) where the matching
`operation_id` is enough and the URI is implicit from the failed
call.

method.description:
HTTP method to use against `href`. Required for entity hypermedia
actions; usually omitted on error-recovery actions where the same
operation is retried with its original method.

operation_id.description:
OpenAPI `operationId` the caller should invoke. Lets an MCP client
resolve the call without parsing `href`.

rel.description:
Link relation describing the action. Closed vocabulary documented
in `specs/api-conventions.md` — examples: `self`, `cancel`, `pause`,
`resume`, `events`, `retry`, `retry-later`, `unarchive`,
`get-existing`, `delete`, `update`.

schema_ref.description:
OpenAPI `$ref` to the request-body schema, when the action takes one
(e.g. `#/components/schemas/UpdateSessionRequest`). Lets a tool-calling
agent fetch the input shape without scanning the whole spec.

label.description: Human-readable label. Safe to render in user-facing messages.

metadata.description:
Free-form metadata attached to this account (caller-defined; opaque to the platform).
Example: `{"team": "support", "cost_center": "ops"}`.

owner_id.description:
Prefixed identifier of the owning principal. Must use the prefix that matches `owner_type`
(see above). Example below pairs with `owner_type = "agent_identity"`.

owner_type.description:
Principal class that owns the account. One of: `user`, `agent_identity`, `organization`.
The prefix on `owner_id` must match this: `user` → `user_…`, `agent_identity` → `identity_…`,
`organization` → `org_…`.

private_key.description:
Private key material for the rail. Stored encrypted; never returned in responses.

public_address.description: Public address on the rail (chain address, account number, etc.). Optional; can be filled in later.

allowed_capabilities.description: Capability IDs this policy permits paid calls for. Empty list means no capability gating.

allowed_hosts.description: HTTP host allowlist for paid outbound calls. Empty list means no host gating.

max_amount_usd_per_day.description: Maximum cumulative amount (USD) per UTC day. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored for forward compatibility; the authority currently checks only `max_amount_usd_per_request`. `None` means no per-day cap.

max_amount_usd_per_request.description: Maximum amount (USD) any single paid request may settle for. **Enforced** by the payment authority at policy selection. `None` means no per-request cap.

max_amount_usd_per_turn.description: Maximum cumulative amount (USD) per agent turn. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored for forward compatibility; the authority currently checks only `max_amount_usd_per_request`. `None` means no per-turn cap.

metadata.description:
Free-form metadata attached to this policy.
Example: `{"owner_team": "ops", "ticket": "OPS-1248"}`.

payment_account_id.description:
Payment account this policy authorizes spending from. Accepts a prefixed `payacct_…`
identifier or a bare UUID.

rail_preference.description: Preferred settlement rails in priority order; the authority picks the first available.

require_approval_above_usd.description: Threshold (USD) above which a request would require explicit human approval. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored for forward compatibility; no approval gate is wired up yet. `None` disables the (future) gate.

subject_id.description:
Prefixed identifier of the bound subject. Must use the prefix matching `subject_type`
(see above). Example below pairs with `subject_type = "agent_identity"`.

subject_type.description:
Class of subject this policy binds to. One of: `user`, `agent_identity`, `agent`, `app`, `session`, `org`.
The prefix on `subject_id` must match: `user`→`user_…`, `agent_identity`→`identity_…`,
`agent`→`agent_…`, `app`→`app_…`, `session`→`session_…`, `org`→`org_…`.

For standard providers, this can be omitted to use the default URL.

Typed credential fields keyed by the driver's declared credential-schema
field names. Validated against the schema and assembled into the stored
credential document. Takes precedence over `api_key` when present.

report that isn't pinned to any dashboard layout.

for the full field breakdown.

ID of the harness for this session (format: harness_{32-hex}).

Mutually exclusive with `harness_name`.

Harness name (e.g. "generic", "deep-research").
Alternative to `harness_id` — looked up by name within the org.
Mutually exclusive with `harness_id`.

Session-level initial files (additive to agent initial_files).

mcpServers.description: Remote MCP servers scoped to this session only.

The ID of the LLM model to use for this session.

Network access list controlling which hosts/URLs this session can reach.
Merged with harness and agent layers (allowed: intersect, blocked: union).
Example shape is defined on `NetworkAccessList`.

Request-level parallel tool calling preference (EVE-598). `true` signals
the provider that parallel tool calls are wanted; `false` requests at
most one tool call per turn and forces serial execution. Omit to inherit

Optional session-level system prompt override.

Attach this session to an existing Workspace (format: `wsp_<32-hex>`)
instead of auto-creating a default per-session workspace. The workspace
must exist in the caller's org and be `active`. Lets multiple sessions
share one working filesystem. Omit for the default 1:1 behavior.

ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error response.

Wire shape is [RFC 9457 Problem Details](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457):
every error response includes `title` and `status`, and may include
`detail`, `code`, `allowed_actions`, `retry_after_seconds`, `instance`,
and `type`. The content type is rewritten to `application/problem+json`
by [`problem_json_content_type`].

allowed_actions.description: Recovery actions the caller can take next.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

retry_after_seconds.description: Seconds the caller should wait before retrying (429 / transient 503).

status.description: HTTP status code; mirrors the response status line.

title.description: Short, human-readable summary of the problem (e.g. "Not Found").

type.description: RFC 9457 problem type URI. Optional; identifies the problem class.

Event.description:
Standard event following the Everruns event protocol.

All events have a consistent structure:
- `id`: Unique event identifier (format: event_{32-hex})
- `type`: Event type in dot notation (e.g., "input.message", "reason.started")
- `ts`: ISO 8601 timestamp with millisecond precision
- `session_id`: Session this event belongs to (format: session_{32-hex})
- `context`: Correlation context for tracing
- `data`: Event-specific payload (typed via EventData enum)
- `metadata`: Optional arbitrary metadata
- `tags`: Optional list of tags for filtering

data.description:
Event-specific payload. The schema depends on the event type.
See EventData documentation for the mapping of type to data schema.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata for the event

session_id.description: Session this event belongs to (format: session_{32-hex})

tags.description: Tags for filtering and categorization

EventContext.description:
Context for event correlation and tracing

Uses OpenTelemetry-style trace/span IDs for observability correlation:
- `trace_id`: Root of the trace (typically the turn_id string)
- `span_id`: This event's unique span identifier
- `parent_span_id`: The parent span's identifier for hierarchical linking

input_message_id.description: User message that triggered this turn

parent_span_id.description:
Parent span ID for hierarchical linking (OTel-style).
Links this span to its parent in the trace hierarchy.

span_id.description:
This event's span ID for observability (OTel-style).
Uniquely identifies this span within the trace.

trace_id.description:
Trace ID for observability (OTel-style). Groups related spans into a single trace.
For agent turns, this is typically the turn_id string.

Execution phase for assistant messages in multi-step tool-calling flows.

Providers that natively support phases (OpenAI GPT-5.x) send the phase value
directly in the API request. For providers without native support (Anthropic,
Gemini), the phase is still tracked internally and derived from state in the
ReasonAtom, but is not sent to the provider API.

Serialized as lowercase strings for backward compatibility with existing
persisted messages: `"commentary"` and `"final_answer"`.

Legacy values `"in_progress"` and `"completed"` are accepted during
deserialization for backward compatibility.

keeps the API spec stable. The frontend layers its own typed view on top.

of `GitDiff.entries`).

insertions.description:
Total added lines summed across all files (the `+` count in a
unified diff).

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Request-level parallel tool calling preference (EVE-598).

`None` (default) preserves provider defaults. `Some(true)` signals the
provider that parallel tool calls are wanted; `Some(false)` requests at
most one tool call per turn and forces serial execution. Merged across
harness/agent/session layers (overlay wins).

System prompt that defines the harness's base behavior.

Forms the foundation of the prompt stack. Optional: when absent the
harness contributes no base prompt, so the effective prompt comes
entirely from the parent harness (if any), the agent, the session, and
capability contributions. Empty/whitespace-only values normalize to
`None`.

claimed_tasks.description: Tasks currently claimed by a worker (gauge).

completed_tasks.description: Cumulative count of tasks that completed successfully (monotonic counter).

completed_workflows.description: Cumulative count of workflows that completed successfully (monotonic counter).

current_load.description: Total tasks currently in flight across all workers.

dlq_size.description: Size of the dead-letter queue (gauge). High values indicate stuck activities.

started_tasks.description: Cumulative count of tasks claimed at least once (monotonic counter).

started_workflows.description: Cumulative count of workflows that started (monotonic counter).

total_capacity.description: Sum of `max_concurrency` across all workers (the upper bound on concurrent task execution).

total_workers.description: Total number of workers registered (heartbeating in the last window).

workers_accepting.description: Number of workers currently accepting new task assignments (subset of `active_workers`; drains/backpressure excluded).

ListResponse_Event.description:
Response wrapper for list endpoints.
All list endpoints return responses wrapped in a `data` field.

items.description:
Standard event following the Everruns event protocol.

All events have a consistent structure:
- `id`: Unique event identifier (format: event_{32-hex})
- `type`: Event type in dot notation (e.g., "input.message", "reason.started")
- `ts`: ISO 8601 timestamp with millisecond precision
- `session_id`: Session this event belongs to (format: session_{32-hex})
- `context`: Correlation context for tracing
- `data`: Event-specific payload (typed via EventData enum)
- `metadata`: Optional arbitrary metadata
- `tags`: Optional list of tags for filtering

Event-specific payload. The schema depends on the event type.
See EventData documentation for the mapping of type to data schema.

allowed_actions.description:

LlmCompactionInfo.description:
Information about context compaction performed before LLM generation

When the conversation context exceeds the model's limit, compaction is
automatically triggered to compress the context before retrying.

input_tokens_after.description: Number of input tokens after compaction

input_tokens_before.description: Number of input tokens before compaction

LlmGenerationData.description:
Data for llm.generation event

Emitted after each LLM API call to provide full visibility into
the messages sent to the model and the response received.

messages.description: Messages sent to the LLM (including system prompt)

metadata.description: Metadata about the generation

tools.description: Tools available to the LLM for this generation

oneOf.description:
Compaction information if context was compressed before generation
Occurs when the conversation context exceeded the model's limit

duration_ms.description: Duration of the generation in milliseconds

finish_reasons.description:
Finish reasons from the LLM (e.g., ["stop"], ["tool_calls"])
Required for gen-ai semantic conventions

oneOf.description: Request-side driver options that were enabled for this generation.

response_id.description:
Unique response identifier from the LLM provider
Required for gen-ai semantic conventions

oneOf.description:
Retry information if rate limit retries occurred
Contains number of retries and total wait time

time_to_first_token_ms.description: Time to first token in milliseconds (streaming latency)

oneOf.description: Token usage statistics

tool_calls.description: Tool calls requested by the model

LlmPromptCacheInfo.description: Request-side prompt cache settings for an LLM generation.

enabled.description: Whether prompt caching was enabled on the request.

provider_mode.description: Provider-specific prompt-cache mode used by the driver.

strategy.description: Strategy used to enable prompt caching.

LlmRequestOptions.description:
Request options applied to an LLM generation.

These fields capture request-side intent such as prompt caching or deferred
tool loading. They complement `usage`, which captures what actually happened.

metadata.description:
General request metadata passed to the LLM provider for tracking and observability.
Includes embedder-supplied labels merged with system tracking keys (session_id, turn_id, etc.).

oneOf.description: Prompt caching configuration for this request.

provider_options.description: Provider-specific request options that do not warrant dedicated fields.

oneOf.description: Deferred tool-loading configuration for this request.

LlmRetryInfo.description: Information about rate limit retries during LLM generation

attempts.description: Number of retry attempts made (0 = succeeded on first try)

total_wait_ms.description: Total time spent waiting between retries in milliseconds

LlmToolSearchInfo.description: Request-side tool_search settings for an LLM generation.

enabled.description: Whether tool_search was enabled on the request.

threshold.description: Minimum number of tools before deferred loading activates.

action.description: Action to take on the sandbox (`reset`, `delete`, etc.).

deleted.description: Whether the action deleted the sandbox (`true` after a successful `delete`).

display_name.description: Human-readable sandbox label.

exists.description: Whether a sandbox instance still exists after the action.

oneOf.description: Current sandbox lifecycle status after the action.

workspace_path.description: Absolute path of the sandbox workspace root.

ManualMemorySourceResponse.description: Response body for manual memory source.

McpErrorCategory.description:
Broad-strokes routing hint sitting alongside the precise [`McpErrorCode`].
The categories are stable enough that an LLM can pick a recovery
strategy from this field alone (e.g. retry transients with backoff,
surface validation errors to the user, escalate auth failures).

McpErrorCode.description:
Closed vocabulary of error codes for Everruns' own MCP `tools/call`
execute path. Surfaces in [`McpExecuteError::code`] so LLM toolcallers
can branch on a machine-readable value instead of regexing prose.

New variants are a spec change. SDKs should treat any value they don't
recognise as `unknown` (forward-compat) — serde's `#[serde(other)]`
catch-all enables that on the deserialize side.

McpExecuteError.description:
Typed structured-error envelope returned by Everruns' MCP `tools/call`
execute path. Serialized into the MCP `structuredContent` field on
error responses so the legacy `content[0].text` channel stays
backward-compatible; new SDKs prefer the typed envelope.

See `specs/mcp.md` for the error contract.

cause_chain.description:
Chain of upstream error messages, oldest cause first. Useful for
debugging; SDKs should not treat this as machine-readable.

code.description:
Machine-readable error code. Closed vocabulary; SDKs that see an
unrecognised value should map it to `unknown`.

hint.description: Short, agent-readable recovery hint. Free-form; one or two sentences.

message.description:
Human-readable error message. Mirrors the legacy
`content[0].text` string for backward compat.

retry_after_seconds.description:
Seconds the caller should wait before retrying. Set on
`tool_timeout`, `quota_exceeded`, and upstream-unreachable cases
when the server has a concrete back-off hint.

retryable.description:
`true` when the same call is worth retrying. Distinct from
`category == "transient"` because a server may know about a
non-transient retry path (e.g. a transient `Internal`).

McpProtocolMode.description:
Per-server policy for which MCP protocol era the client uses.

`Auto` (the default) probes the server and adapts — it tries the stateless
RC path first and transparently falls back to the stateful handshake when a
server demands it, so a single configuration speaks to legacy, current, and
RC servers without operator action. The pinned variants skip negotiation
when an operator knows a server's era (or to work around a server that
mis-signals it).

McpServer.description:
MCP Server configuration.
Represents a remote MCP server that can provide tools and resources.

api_key_set.description: Whether an API key has been configured.

archived_at.description: Timestamp when the MCP server was archived.

auth_mode.description: Authentication mode for this MCP server.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the MCP server was created.

deleted_at.description: Timestamp when the MCP server was deleted.

description.description: Human-readable description of the MCP server.

headers.description:
Additional HTTP headers for authentication.
Keys are header names, values are header values.

name.description: Display name of the MCP server.

oauth_provider_id.description: Stable provider id used for user-scoped OAuth connections.

protocol_mode.description: Protocol-era adoption policy for the MCP client (`auto` negotiates).

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the MCP server.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the MCP server was last updated.

url.description: URL of the MCP server endpoint.

McpServerStatus.description:
MCP Server lifecycle status.
- `active`: Server is available for use
- `disabled`: Server is disabled and not used
- `archived`: Server is hidden from listings and cannot be modified or assigned
- `deleted`: Server is a tombstone kept only for historical references

McpToolAnnotations.description:
MCP tool annotations as defined by the MCP specification.
All fields are optional booleans following the MCP convention.

archived_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was archived, if any (RFC 3339).

created_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was created (RFC 3339).

deleted_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was soft-deleted, if any (RFC 3339).

description.example: Living design documents synced from GitHub

is_readonly.description: Whether the memory is mounted read-only into sessions. Read-only memories accept no writes from the session sandbox.

last_sync_error.description: Most recent sync error message; cleared on the next successful sync.
last_sync_error.example: ssh: connect to host github.com port 22: Connection timed out

last_synced_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful sync (RFC 3339). `None` if never synced.

name.description: Human-readable name. Safe to render in user-facing messages.

source.description: Source-specific configuration (git remote, github repo, manual upload).

source_type.description: Source kind discriminator (`manual`, `git`, `github`). Determines which `source` variant is populated.

status.description: Current lifecycle status.

sync_status.description: Current sync status (`idle`, `syncing`, `succeeded`, `failed`). Only meaningful when `source_type` is `git` or `github`.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was last updated (RFC 3339).

content.description: Message content as array of content parts (text, images, tool calls, tool results)

oneOf.description: Runtime controls (model, reasoning, etc.)

created_at.description: Timestamp when the message was created

oneOf.description: External actor identity (for messages from external channels like Slack)

metadata.description: Message-level metadata

oneOf.description:
Execution phase for this message.

Helps LLMs distinguish between intermediate working commentary and completed
answers in multi-step tool-calling flows. Only set on agent (assistant) messages.
Providers with native phase support (OpenAI GPT-5.x) send this value in the API
request; others derive it from state but don't send it to the provider.

thinking.description:
Thinking content from extended thinking models (Anthropic Claude)
This is the model's chain-of-thought reasoning before producing the response.
Must be included in subsequent API calls when thinking is enabled.

thinking_signature.description:
Cryptographic signature for thinking content (Anthropic Claude)
Required when sending thinking back in subsequent API calls.

message.description: Message text dispatched to the agent.

claimed_tasks.description: Number of tasks currently claimed by a worker (gauge).

load_percentage.description: Aggregate worker load as a percentage of total `max_concurrency` (0.0-100.0).

pending_tasks.description: Number of tasks waiting to be claimed by a worker (gauge).

pending_workflows.description: Number of workflows waiting to be claimed by a worker (gauge).

tasks_completed_total.description: Cumulative count of tasks completed successfully since process start (monotonic counter).

tasks_failed_total.description: Cumulative count of tasks that failed or were sent to the DLQ (monotonic counter).

tasks_started_total.description: Cumulative count of tasks claimed at least once (monotonic counter).

timestamp.description: Sampling timestamp for this data point (RFC 3339).

workflows_completed_total.description: Cumulative count of workflows that completed successfully (monotonic counter).

workflows_failed_total.description: Cumulative count of workflows that ended in failure (monotonic counter).

workflows_started_total.description: Cumulative count of workflows that started (monotonic counter).

Metrics time series response.

are expected so consumers can aggregate or label appropriately.

When >1, these metrics represent only this instance's view.

Disabled models stay visible in raw list endpoints (so admins can re-enable them) but cannot be used in active sessions or as a session/agent default.

model_id.description: Provider-side model identifier as sent on the wire (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4`).

When present, the base cost fields apply up to the tier threshold,
and each tier's costs apply for tokens beyond that threshold.

context.description: Maximum context window size in tokens

input.description: Maximum input tokens (if different from context - output)

LLM Model Profile describing model capabilities

NOTE: Currently only includes profiles for:
- OpenAI: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o1, o1-mini, o1-pro, o3-mini
- Anthropic: claude-3-5-sonnet, claude-3-5-haiku, claude-3-opus, claude-3-sonnet, claude-3-haiku, claude-sonnet-4, claude-opus-4

Additional model profiles can be added as needed.

Supported modalities

Whether the model supports native execution phases ("commentary" / "final_answer").
When true, the driver sends the `phase` field on assistant messages in the wire format.
Currently supported by GPT-5.4 and newer via OpenAI Responses API.

When true, the driver can use namespaces and defer_loading to reduce

NetworkAccessList.description:
Network access list controlling which hosts/URLs an agent session can reach.

- `allowed`: if non-empty, only URLs matching these patterns are permitted.
- `blocked`: URLs matching these patterns are always denied (takes precedence over allowed).

Pattern format:
- `example.com` — exact domain match (any port, any path)
- `*.example.com` — domain and all subdomains
- `https://example.com/api/` — exact URL prefix (scheme + host + path)

allowed.description:
Allowed host patterns. If non-empty, only matching URLs are permitted.
An empty list means "no restriction from this layer" (inherit parent).

blocked.description: Blocked host patterns. Always denied, even if matched by `allowed`.

OkfFileInput.description:
A single inline bundle file, for callers that send files as JSON rather
than a tarball.

pruned.description: Entries removed because `prune` was set and they were absent from the bundle.

warnings.description: Non-fatal issues (broken docs, oversized bodies). OKF consumers degrade gracefully.

org_enabled.description: Whether the organization has opted in.

system_enabled.description: Whether the deployment allows this flag (env / grade).

base_harness_id.description: Base harness used when session creation omits harness_id.

created_at.description: When the organization was created

default_harness_id.description: Default harness to preselect in the UI.

default_model_id.description: Default LLM model for the organization.

default_provider_per_service.description:
Org-level default provider per service (EVE-569), keyed by service kind.
Empty when no org defaults are configured.

name.description: Display name

updated_at.description: When the organization was last updated

error_code.description: Stable error code for user-facing failures surfaced as assistant text.

error_disclosure.description:
Error-disclosure mode applied to `error_code`/`error_fields`
("generic" | "standard" | "detailed"). Tracking metadata; absent for
non-error messages and for paths that predate disclosure modes.

error_fields.description: Structured interpolation fields for localized error rendering.

oneOf.description: Metadata about the model used
Token usage

OutputMessageDeltaData.description:
Data for output.message.delta event

Incremental text update during LLM generation. Events are batched (~100ms)
to reduce volume while providing real-time feedback.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this delta belongs to

OutputMessageReplacedData.description:
Data for `output.message.replaced` event.

Emitted between the last (suppressed) `output.message.delta` and the final
`output.message.completed`. Tells the client to discard everything it has
accumulated for `turn_id` and use `replacement` as the assistant message
text. The original model output is never persisted or replayed.

guardrail_capability_id.description:
Stable ID of the capability that contributed the guardrail
(e.g. `"prompt_canary_guardrail"`).

guardrail_id.description: Stable ID of the guardrail itself (e.g. `"prompt_canary"`).

reason_code.description:
Stable machine-readable reason code (e.g. `"system_prompt_leak"`).
Clients localize their copy from this rather than the human text.

replacement.description: Replacement text shown to the user and stored as the assistant message.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this replacement belongs to.

OutputMessageStartedData.description:
Data for output.message.started event

Emitted when the LLM starts generating a response. UI can show a
"thinking" indicator until output.message.delta or output.message.completed events arrive.

iteration.description:
Current iteration number within this turn (1-based).
Useful for UI to show progress during multi-step tool-calling flows.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this output belongs to

PaginatedResponse_Session.description:
Response wrapper for paginated list endpoints.
Includes pagination metadata along with the data array.

`next_url` and `prev_url` are populated by the [`decorate_pagination_links`]
middleware after the handler returns, so handlers don't need to thread the
request URL into every construction site.

items.description:
Session - instance of agentic loop execution.
A session represents a single conversation with an agent.

active_schedule_count.description:
Number of active (enabled) schedules for this session.
Populated when the session is fetched for API responses.

agent_id.description: ID of the agent working in this session (format: agent_{32-hex}). Optional.

agent_identity_id.description: Optional resident agent identity for unattended/background execution.

agent_version_id.description: Immutable agent version captured when the session was created or rebound.

blueprint_config.description:
Validated config passed by host at blueprint spawn time.
Example: `{"target_repo": "acme/everruns"}`.

blueprint_id.description:
Blueprint ID. When set, reason_activity and act_activity build RuntimeAgent
from the blueprint definition instead of from harness_id/agent_id.

capabilities.description:
Session-level capabilities (additive to agent capabilities).
Applied after agent capabilities when building RuntimeAgent.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the session was created.

oneOf.description: Effective human owner summary.

features.description:
Aggregated UI features from all active capabilities (harness + agent + session).
Computed at read time from the capability registry.
Known features: "file_system", "schedules", "secrets", "key_value",
"sql_database", "leased_resources".

finished_at.description: Timestamp when the session finished (completed or failed).

forked_from_sequence.description:
Parent event sequence the fork was taken at (the fork point). NULL unless
this session is a fork.

forked_from_session_id.description:
Session this one was forked from. NULL for sessions that were not forked.
Distinct from `parent_session_id` (subagent nesting): forking is a
user-initiated "branch from here" relationship.

harness_id.description: ID of the harness for this session (format: harness_{32-hex}).

hints.description:
Session-level client hints — arbitrary key-value pairs declared by the
client at session creation time. These are defaults for every turn;
per-message `controls.hints` override these key-by-key (shallow merge).

Examples: `{"setup_connection": true, "rich_media": true}`

id.description: Unique identifier for the session (format: session_{32-hex}).

initial_files.description:
Session-level initial files (additive to agent initial_files).
Files with matching paths override agent/harness files; new paths are appended.

is_pinned.description:
Whether this session is pinned by the current user.
Only populated when the request has an authenticated user context.

locale.description: Locale for localized agent behavior and formatting (BCP 47, e.g. `uk-UA`).

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of LLM iterations per turn for this session.

mcpServers.description: Remote MCP servers scoped to this session only.

model_id.description:
LLM model ID to use for this session (format: model_{32-hex}).
Overrides the agent's default model if set.

oneOf.description:
Network access list controlling which hosts/URLs this session can reach.
Merged with harness and agent layers (allowed: intersect, blocked: union).

organization_id.description: Organization this session belongs to (format: org_{32-hex}).

output_preview.description: Preview text from the last assistant response (truncated).
output_preview.example: Here is a Q3 plan covering the three pillars we discussed...

oneOf.description: Owning principal summary.

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Request-level parallel tool calling preference (EVE-598).

`None` (default) preserves provider defaults. `Some(true)` signals the
provider that parallel tool calls are wanted; `Some(false)` requests at
most one tool call per turn and forces serial execution. Merged across
harness/agent/session layers (overlay wins).

parent_session_id.description:
Parent session that spawned this subagent. NULL for top-level sessions.
Used as the nesting guard in spawn_subagent.

preview.description: Preview text from the first user message (truncated).
preview.example: Help me draft the Q3 marketing plan

resolved_owner_user_id.description: Denormalized effective human owner of the owning principal lineage.

started_at.description: Timestamp when the session started executing.

status.description: Current execution status of the session.

system_prompt.description:
Session-level system prompt override.
Prepended to the agent's system prompt when building RuntimeAgent.

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering sessions.

tools.description: Client-side tools for this session (additive to agent tools).

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the session was last updated.

oneOf.description: Cumulative token usage for all LLM calls in this session.

workspace_id.description:
Workspace this session is attached to (format: wsp_{32-hex}). Owns the
session's virtual filesystem. For the default 1:1 case this mirrors the
session id, but clients should read it here rather than deriving it.

data.description: Array of items returned by the list operation.

limit.description: Maximum number of items per page.

next_url.description: Absolute URL for the next page, with `offset` advanced by `limit`. Omitted from the response (field absent) when this is the last page.

prev_url.description: Absolute URL for the previous page, with `offset` rolled back by `limit`. Omitted from the response (field absent) when this is the first page.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query (across all pages).

allOf.description:
Public capability information (without internal details)
This is what gets returned from the API
Named CapabilityInfo to distinguish from the Capability trait

agent_count.description: Number of active agents referencing this capability in the org.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema for capability-specific per-agent config.

config_ui_schema.description: react-jsonschema-form uiSchema hints for rendering config_schema.

dependencies.description:
IDs of capabilities that this capability depends on.
When this capability is selected, its dependencies are automatically included.

description.description: Description of what this capability provides
description.example: Read, write, edit, list, grep, delete, and stat files in the session workspace.

docs_slug.description: Slug under https://dev.everruns.com/capabilities/ when public docs exist.

features.description:
UI feature strings this capability contributes to.
Multiple capabilities can contribute the same feature.

harness_count.description: Number of active harnesses referencing this capability in the org.

is_guardrail.description:
Whether this capability is a guardrail (constrains agent behavior

initial_files.description: Starter files copied into each new session for this agent.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of LLM iterations per turn for this agent.

name.description: Name, unique per org (e.g. "customer-support").

oneOf.description:

tools.description:

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the agent was last updated.

oneOf.description: Cumulative token usage across all sessions for this agent.

session_count.description: Number of sessions using this resource.

allOf.description:
Session - instance of agentic loop execution.
A session represents a single conversation with an agent.

active_schedule_count.description:
Number of active (enabled) schedules for this session.
Populated when the session is fetched for API responses.

agent_id.description: ID of the agent working in this session (format: agent_{32-hex}). Optional.

agent_identity_id.description: Optional resident agent identity for unattended/background execution.

agent_version_id.description: Immutable agent version captured when the session was created or rebound.

blueprint_config.description:
Validated config passed by host at blueprint spawn time.
Example: `{"target_repo": "acme/everruns"}`.

blueprint_id.description:
Blueprint ID. When set, reason_activity and act_activity build RuntimeAgent
from the blueprint definition instead of from harness_id/agent_id.

capabilities.description:
Session-level capabilities (additive to agent capabilities).
Applied after agent capabilities when building RuntimeAgent.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the session was created.

oneOf.description: Effective human owner summary.

features.description:
Aggregated UI features from all active capabilities (harness + agent + session).
Computed at read time from the capability registry.
Known features: "file_system", "schedules", "secrets", "key_value",
"sql_database", "leased_resources".

finished_at.description: Timestamp when the session finished (completed or failed).

forked_from_sequence.description:
Parent event sequence the fork was taken at (the fork point). NULL unless
this session is a fork.

forked_from_session_id.description:
Session this one was forked from. NULL for sessions that were not forked.
Distinct from `parent_session_id` (subagent nesting): forking is a
user-initiated "branch from here" relationship.

harness_id.description: ID of the harness for this session (format: harness_{32-hex}).

hints.description:
Session-level client hints — arbitrary key-value pairs declared by the
client at session creation time. These are defaults for every turn;
per-message `controls.hints` override these key-by-key (shallow merge).

Examples: `{"setup_connection": true, "rich_media": true}`

id.description: Unique identifier for the session (format: session_{32-hex}).

initial_files.description:
Session-level initial files (additive to agent initial_files).
Files with matching paths override agent/harness files; new paths are appended.

is_pinned.description:
Whether this session is pinned by the current user.
Only populated when the request has an authenticated user context.

locale.description: Locale for localized agent behavior and formatting (BCP 47, e.g. `uk-UA`).

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of LLM iterations per turn for this session.

mcpServers.description: Remote MCP servers scoped to this session only.

model_id.description:
LLM model ID to use for this session (format: model_{32-hex}).
Overrides the agent's default model if set.

oneOf.description:
Network access list controlling which hosts/URLs this session can reach.
Merged with harness and agent layers (allowed: intersect, blocked: union).

organization_id.description: Organization this session belongs to (format: org_{32-hex}).

output_preview.description: Preview text from the last assistant response (truncated).
output_preview.example: Here is a Q3 plan covering the three pillars we discussed...

oneOf.description: Owning principal summary.

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Request-level parallel tool calling preference (EVE-598).

`None` (default) preserves provider defaults. `Some(true)` signals the
provider that parallel tool calls are wanted; `Some(false)` requests at
most one tool call per turn and forces serial execution. Merged across
harness/agent/session layers (overlay wins).

parent_session_id.description:
Parent session that spawned this subagent. NULL for top-level sessions.
Used as the nesting guard in spawn_subagent.

preview.description: Preview text from the first user message (truncated).
preview.example: Help me draft the Q3 marketing plan

resolved_owner_user_id.description: Denormalized effective human owner of the owning principal lineage.

started_at.description: Timestamp when the session started executing.

status.description: Current execution status of the session.

system_prompt.description:
Session-level system prompt override.
Prepended to the agent's system prompt when building RuntimeAgent.

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering sessions.

tools.description: Client-side tools for this session (additive to agent tools).

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the session was last updated.

oneOf.description: Cumulative token usage for all LLM calls in this session.

workspace_id.description:
Workspace this session is attached to (format: wsp_{32-hex}). Owns the
session's virtual filesystem. For the default 1:1 case this mirrors the
session id, but clients should read it here rather than deriving it.

PaymentAccount.description:
A payment account — the org-scoped source of funds for paid agent calls.
Each account binds an owning principal (user, agent identity, or org)
to one settlement rail and tracks its provisioning lifecycle.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this account was created (RFC 3339).

label.description: Human-readable label for this account. Safe to render in user-facing messages.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata attached to this account (caller-defined; opaque to the platform).

owner_id.description: Prefixed identifier of the owning principal (e.g. `user_…`, `agent_…`, `org_…`).

owner_type.description: Principal class that owns this account (user, agent identity, or organization).

public_address.description: Public address on the rail (chain address, account number, etc.). Optional; `None` until provisioning completes.

rail.description: Settlement rail this account operates on.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this account.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this account was last updated (RFC 3339).

PaymentAttempt.description:
A single paid-call settlement attempt — the durable record of one
authorization+settlement cycle issued through the payment authority.
Persisted regardless of outcome so failed attempts remain auditable.

capability.description: Capability ID that originated this paid call.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this attempt was created (RFC 3339).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the charge (typically `USD`).

error_message.description: Human-readable error message when `status` is `failed`; `None` otherwise.

operation.description: Capability-specific operation name that originated this paid call.

oneOf.description: Payment account that settled (or attempted to settle) this attempt. `None` if no account could be resolved.
Settlement rail actually used. `None` if the attempt failed before rail selection.

receipt.description: Rail-specific receipt payload (transaction id, block reference, signature, etc.).

request_hash.description: Stable hash of the outbound request used to detect replays. `None` when not applicable.

session_id.description: Session that initiated the paid call, if any.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this attempt.

target_url.description: Destination URL of the paid outbound call.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this attempt was last updated (RFC 3339).

PaymentOwnerType.description: Principal class that owns a payment account.

PaymentPolicy.description:
A payment policy — the binding between a paying account and a subject
(agent identity, session) that controls which paid calls are
authorized and at what spend caps.

allowed_capabilities.description: Capability IDs this policy permits paid calls for. Empty list means no capability gating.

allowed_hosts.description: HTTP host allowlist for paid outbound calls. Empty list means no host gating.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this policy was created (RFC 3339).

max_amount_usd_per_day.description: Maximum cumulative amount (USD) per UTC day. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored on the policy for forward compatibility; the payment authority currently checks only `max_amount_usd_per_request`. `None` means no per-day cap.

max_amount_usd_per_request.description: Maximum amount (USD) any single paid request may settle for. **Enforced** by the payment authority at policy selection. `None` means no per-request cap.

max_amount_usd_per_turn.description: Maximum cumulative amount (USD) per agent turn. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored on the policy for forward compatibility; the payment authority currently checks only `max_amount_usd_per_request`. `None` means no per-turn cap.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata attached to this policy.

payment_account_id.description: Payment account this policy authorizes spending from.

rail_preference.description: Preferred settlement rails in priority order; the authority picks the first available.

require_approval_above_usd.description: Threshold (USD) above which a request would require explicit human approval. **Advisory only — not yet enforced.** Stored on the policy for forward compatibility; no approval gate is wired up yet. `None` disables the (future) gate.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this policy.

subject_id.description: Prefixed identifier of the bound subject.

subject_type.description: Class of subject this policy binds to (e.g. `agent_identity`, `session`).

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this policy was last updated (RFC 3339).

PaymentRail.description: Payment rail used to settle a machine payment.

PaymentStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of a payment account, policy, or attempt. The shared
vocabulary keeps account/policy admin and attempt settlement on the
same status taxonomy.

PostTaskMessageBody.description: Request body for posting an inbound task message.

in_reply_to.description: Input request ID this message answers, when applicable.

PreviewAgentRequest.description: Request to preview the final agent shape with capabilities applied

capabilities.description: Capabilities to apply with per-agent configuration.

mcpServers.description: Remote MCP servers scoped to the previewed agent.

system_prompt.description: The base system prompt (before capability additions)
system_prompt.example: You are a helpful customer support agent.

tools.description: Client-side tools to include in the preview.

PreviewHarnessRequest.description: Request to preview harness shape with capabilities applied

capabilities.description: Capability configurations to layer onto the preview. Empty list means none.

mcpServers.description:
MCP servers scoped to this preview, keyed by scope (`shared` / per-agent / etc.).
Use the camelCase key `mcpServers` (preferred) or the snake_case alias `mcp_servers`. Empty by default.

parent_harness_id.description:
Parent harness to extend. When set, its prompt, capabilities, and MCP servers are
merged with the fields in this request before rendering.

system_prompt.description:
System prompt to render as the base prompt for the preview. Optional:
omit to preview a harness that contributes no base prompt of its own.
system_prompt.example: You are a research assistant.

PrincipalKind.description:
Class of principal that can hold permissions or own resources. `system`
is reserved for platform-internal callers and is never minted via the
public API.

PrincipalSummary.description:
Compact view of a principal — id + kind + the subject-id pointer back
into the user/agent-identity row. Used wherever a full `Principal`
would be redundant (e.g. as a sub-field of a session or audit record).

name.description: Human-readable name. Safe to render in user-facing messages.

ProjectorRunQuery.description:
Query parameters for a manual `POST /v1/reports/projector/run` invocation —
the cap on how many outbox rows one run is allowed to claim.

limit.description: Maximum number of items returned in this page.

ProjectorRunResult.description:
Outcome of one projector run — how many outbox rows it claimed,
completed, and failed. Returned by manual `POST /v1/reports/projector/run`
calls and useful for backfill scripting.

claimed.description: Number of outbox rows claimed by this run.

completed.description: Number of claimed rows that completed successfully.

failed.description: Number of claimed rows that failed and will be retried (or moved to `failed` after retry limit).

Provider.description:
LLM Provider entity (API keys never exposed)
Note: This is the entity struct, separate from the Provider trait in llm.rs

api_key_set.description: Whether an API key is configured. The key itself is never returned.

base_url.description: Custom base URL for self-hosted / proxied providers. `None` means use the provider's default endpoint.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this provider was created (RFC 3339).

last_synced_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful model sync from the provider's API (RFC 3339).

name.description: Human-readable provider name. Safe to render in user-facing messages.

provider_type.description: Provider implementation type (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.).

status.description: Current lifecycle status of this provider.

oneOf.description:
Resolved trace/observability link configuration: the driver's default
templates overlaid with this provider's stored overrides. `None` when the
driver exposes no dashboard and the org configured nothing.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this provider was last updated (RFC 3339).

ProviderTraceConfig.description:
Configuration for linking from the chat UI to a provider's observability
dashboard ("trace"/"logs").

This is provider-agnostic: any driver with a dashboard can supply default
templates (see [`DriverId::default_trace_templates`]), and an org enables
links per provider once it has confirmed logging is on for that account.
URL templates support the `{response_id}`, `{session_id}`, `{turn_id}` and
`{model}` placeholders, so the same mechanism works for OpenRouter today and
for third-party observability backends (Langfuse, Helicone, ...) via an
override.

enabled.description:
Whether trace links should be shown for this provider. Defaults to
`false`: vendors typically do not retain trace content unless logging is
explicitly enabled, so the org opts in once that is set up.

generation_url_template.description:
URL template for a single generation's trace, e.g.
`"https://openrouter.ai/logs?id={response_id}"`.

session_url_template.description:
URL template for a session's grouped trace, e.g.
`"https://openrouter.ai/logs"`.

ProvidersConfigResponse.description:
Provider resource config: caller policies plus the credential schemas the UI
renders per driver.

drivers.description: Per-driver credential schemas, ordered by driver id.

policies.description: Map of policy ID → whether the caller satisfies it.

duration_ms.description: Duration of the reason phase in milliseconds

oneOf.description: Token usage from the LLM call

ReasonItemData.description:
Data for `reason.item` event.

Durable record of an opaque assistant reasoning response item (e.g., OpenAI
Responses API reasoning items). Carries provider-supplied opaque artifacts
and curated summary text only. Plaintext hidden chain-of-thought is never
persisted in this event — emitters must strip any plaintext reasoning
content before constructing it.

encrypted_content.description: Provider-encrypted reasoning context, if supplied. Opaque to consumers.

item_id.description: Provider-assigned identifier for the reasoning item.

model.description: Model identifier reported by the provider, if known.

provider.description: Provider that produced the reasoning item (e.g., "openai").

summary.description:
Safe summary text segments curated by the provider. Never includes
plaintext reasoning content.

token_count.description: Per-item reasoning token count, when the provider reports one.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this reasoning item belongs to.

ReasonRecoveredData.description:
Data for the `reason.recovered` event (EVE-532).

Emitted by `ReasonAtom` when it detects an in-flight partial assistant
message from a previous worker execution and applies the ContinuePartial
recovery policy.

accumulated_len.description: Character length of the persisted accumulated text.

turn_id.description: Turn ID the partial belonged to.

oneOf.description: Agent ID being used (optional)
Metadata about the model being used

ReasonThinkingCompletedData.description:
Data for reason.thinking.completed event

Emitted when extended thinking completes and the model transitions
to producing the final response. Contains the complete thinking content.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this thinking belongs to

ReasonThinkingDeltaData.description:
Data for reason.thinking.delta event (extended thinking content from models like Claude)

This event streams incremental thinking/reasoning content from models that support
extended thinking mode (e.g., Claude with thinking enabled). The thinking content
represents the model's chain-of-thought reasoning before producing the final response.

accumulated.description: Accumulated thinking text so far (convenience for UI)

delta.description: The thinking delta (new thinking text since last delta)

turn_id.description: Turn ID this delta belongs to (for correlation)

ReasonThinkingStartedData.description:
Data for reason.thinking.started event

Emitted when extended thinking begins during reasoning phase.
This signals the model is using chain-of-thought reasoning.
UI can show a "thinking" indicator.

turn_id.description: Turn ID this thinking belongs to

effort.description: Effort level for reasoning (low, medium, high)

ReasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort level for models that support it

ReasoningEffortConfig.description: Reasoning effort configuration for a model

default.description: Default reasoning effort for this model

values.description: Available reasoning effort values for this model

ReasoningEffortValue.description: Named reasoning effort value for UI display

name.description: Display name (e.g., "Low", "Medium")

RecoveryMode.description: Recovery mode chosen by the ContinuePartial classifier (EVE-532).

RegenerateA2aApiKeyOutput.description:
Output of [`RegenerateA2aApiKeyCmd`] — includes the newly generated
plaintext API key (returned **once**, never persisted) plus the updated
[`AppChannel`].

api_key.description:
New plaintext API key. Persist this — it cannot be recovered later.
The previous key is invalidated immediately.

RegenerateApiEndpointApiKeyOutput.description:
Output of [`RegenerateApiEndpointApiKeyCmd`] — includes the newly generated
plaintext API key (returned **once**, never persisted) plus the updated
[`AppChannel`].

api_key.description:
New plaintext API key. Persist this — it cannot be recovered later.
The previous key is invalidated immediately.

ReportColumn.description:
One column header in a `ReportResult`. The ordered `columns` list
declares the key set of each row in `rows`.

kind.description:
Whether this column is a grouping `dimension` or an aggregate
`measure` — the same distinction made on `ReportQuery`.

name.description: Column name as it appears in `rows`.

ReportColumnKind.description: Whether a `ReportColumn` is a grouping dimension or an aggregate measure.

ReportExport.description:
Serialized export of a report's data, ready to stream to a caller as a
download. Carries the rendered payload plus the MIME/filename metadata
a client needs to save it.

as_of.description: Timestamp the underlying data was materialized (RFC 3339). Useful for "as of" footers.

content.description: Serialized payload as a UTF-8 string. Caller streams this to the client.

content_type.description: MIME type matching `format` (`text/csv` for CSV exports).

filename.description: Suggested filename for the download (includes the extension matching `format`).

freshness_lag_ms.description: How stale the data is relative to `now()`, in milliseconds. `None` when freshness can't be determined.

ReportExportFormat.description:
Output format for a report export. `Csv` emits a header row plus one
row per result; `Json` emits an envelope with the same shape as
`ReportResult`.

ReportFilter.description:
One predicate filter applied to the dataset before aggregation.
Combined with other filters via logical AND.

field.description: Field to filter on. Must be a filter field exposed by the dataset (see `filter_fields` in the catalog).

op.description:
Comparison operator. Determines the expected shape of `value`
(scalar for `eq`/`neq`/`gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`, array for `in`).

value.description:
Comparison value. Type depends on `op`: a scalar for `eq`/`neq`/`gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`,
an array for `in`. Example for `op = in`: `["completed", "failed"]`.

ReportFilterOp.description:
Comparison operator used in a `ReportFilter`. The `In` variant takes a
JSON array as its value; all others take a scalar.

ReportOrderBy.description:
One sort clause applied to the aggregated result. Either `dimension`
OR `measure` is set (mutually exclusive), never both.

dimension.description: Dimension name to sort by. Mutually exclusive with `measure`.

measure.description: Measure name to sort by. Mutually exclusive with `dimension`.

ReportQuery.description:
Semantic query a caller submits to the reporting layer. The backend
compiles this to its native query language, scopes it to the calling
org, and returns a `ReportResult`.

dataset.description: Dataset name to query (see `GET /v1/reports/catalog` for the list of available datasets).

dimensions.description: Columns to group by. Empty list returns one aggregate row.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return (defaults to 100).

measures.description: Aggregations to compute (count, sum, avg, etc.). Empty list returns row counts only.

order_by.description: Sort spec applied after aggregation. Empty list yields unspecified order.

time_range.description: Time window for the query. The dataset selects which timestamp column the range applies to.

ReportResult.description:
Materialized result of a report query — column metadata, rows, and the
freshness of the underlying data.

as_of.description:
Timestamp the underlying data was materialized (RFC 3339). Useful as
an "as of" footer when rendering — distinct from when the query ran.

columns.description:
Column metadata in the same order as the entries of each row in
`rows`. Use the `kind` field to tell dimensions from measures.

freshness_lag_ms.description:
How stale the data is relative to the server's wall clock at query
time, in milliseconds. `None` when freshness can't be determined
(e.g. backends that don't track projector lag).

rows.description:

TokenUsage.description:
Token usage statistics

Tracks token consumption per LLM call including cache tokens for cost
optimization.

# Disjoint bucket convention

Prompt token buckets are **disjoint** (non-overlapping). Drivers normalize
provider wire formats at the boundary so this holds for every provider:

```text
total_prompt = input_tokens + cache_read_tokens + cache_creation_tokens
```

- `input_tokens` — non-cached prompt tokens only.
- `cache_read_tokens` — tokens served from cache, never counted in
  `input_tokens`.
- `cache_creation_tokens` — tokens written to cache, never counted in
  `input_tokens`.

Inclusive providers (OpenAI Responses / Chat Completions, Gemini) report a
prompt count that *includes* cached reads; their drivers subtract the cached
subset so the value stored here is the non-cached remainder. Anthropic /
Bedrock already report disjoint buckets. Cost is therefore uniform across
providers (`input·in + cache_read·cr + cache_creation·cw + output·out`);
consumers must not re-derive a non-cached input by subtracting cache reads.

actual_cost_usd.description:
Actual cost of this generation in USD, as reported by the provider inline
(e.g. OpenRouter's `usage.cost`, which reflects real post-routing/BYOK/cache
pricing). `None` for providers that do not return a cost.

cache_creation_tokens.description: Number of tokens written to cache, disjoint from `input_tokens`

cache_read_tokens.description: Number of tokens read from cache (reduces cost), disjoint from `input_tokens`

effective_cost_usd.description:
Best-effort USD cost for already-aggregated usage. Per-generation usage
leaves this unset and derives the effective cost from actual/estimated.

estimated_cost_usd.description:
Estimated cost of this generation in USD, derived from the model's static
price-table profile. Computed whenever a profile with cost data exists,
independently of `actual_cost_usd`, so estimate-vs-actual drift can be
reconciled. `None` when there is no profile cost data for the model.

Number of non-cached prompt tokens (cached reads/writes are tracked
separately; see the disjoint bucket convention on the struct)

Semantic hints describing a tool's behavioral properties.

Follows the MCP tool annotations convention (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint,
idempotentHint, openWorldHint) plus everruns-specific hints. All fields are
optional booleans — `None` means "unknown/unspecified". Consumers should
treat `None` as the conservative default (e.g., assume not readonly, assume
not idempotent).

These hints are informational — they do not enforce policy. Use `ToolPolicy`
for execution gating (auto vs requires_approval).

capability_id.description:
Capability that contributed this tool definition.

Reporting uses this attribution only as metadata. It must never contain
tool arguments, results, prompts, or any other sensitive payload.

capability_name.description: Human-readable capability name snapshot for reporting.

concurrency_class.description:
Scheduling conflict key. Tool calls within the same act batch that share
a non-empty `concurrency_class` are executed sequentially in arrival
order; calls in different classes (or with no class) run concurrently.

Set this on tools that mutate shared session state so that, e.g., two
file writes or two SQL mutations in one batch do not race. Read-only
tools should leave this `None` so they always parallelize. See
`crate::atoms::tool_scheduler` for how the act scheduler consumes it.

cpu_bound.description:
Tool performs significant CPU-bound or otherwise non-yielding work in
process (e.g. an in-process interpreter). When true, the act scheduler
runs the call on its own task (`tokio::spawn`) so a long CPU burst does
not starve the cooperative polling of I/O-bound tools in the same batch.

Distinct from `long_running`, which describes wall-clock time for
I/O-bound work (those tools yield at await points and need no offload).

destructive.description:
Tool may irreversibly destroy or delete data.
Subset of non-readonly — a tool can be non-readonly (writes) without
being destructive (e.g., create/update operations).

idempotent.description:
Calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments produces the same
effect. Safe to retry on transient failures.

long_running.description:
Tool may take significant time to complete (> ~5s typical).
Useful for clients to show progress indicators and set timeouts.

narration_noun.description:
Entity noun for operation-based narration (e.g. "agent", "harness").
When set, the narration system reads the `operation` argument and
produces verb-based narration like "Created agent: Neon Cartographer"
instead of the generic "Ran Manage Agents".

open_world.description:
Tool interacts with external entities beyond the local system
(network calls, third-party APIs, cloud services).

persist_output.description:
Tool output should be persisted to session VFS before truncation.
When set, the `tool_output_persistence` capability (EVE-222, EVE-245) writes
stdout to `/outputs/{tool_call_id}.stdout` and stderr to
`/outputs/{tool_call_id}.stderr`, injecting `full_output`, `total_lines`,
and `output_files` into the result.

readonly.description:
Tool does not modify any state (read-only queries, lookups).
When true: safe to call speculatively, result can be cached.

requires_secrets.description:
Tool requires API keys, credentials, or other secrets to function.
Useful for UI to show connection prompts and for LLMs to anticipate
authentication failures.

oneOf.description:
Replay-safety class used by the durable Act activity (EVE-530).

Controls what happens when a worker reclaims a stale `running` claim:
`Pure`/`Idempotent` tools are re-executed; `AtMostOnce` tools are
settled as `interrupted` to prevent double side-effects.

`None` is treated conservatively as `AtMostOnce`.

supports_background.description:
Tool supports detached background execution via `spawn_background`.
When true, the tool may be executed asynchronously outside the current
foreground tool call and report status back later.

ToolOutputDeltaData.description:
Data for tool.output.delta event.

Emitted by tools during execution to stream incremental output chunks.
This enables live output rendering (e.g., bash stdout/stderr, command output)
between tool.started and tool.completed. Generic — usable by any tool that
produces streamed output (bashkit, Daytona exec, subagent speech, etc.).

The consumer accumulates deltas by tool_call_id for display. The final
tool.completed result is authoritative — deltas are informational only.

tool_call_id.description: Tool call ID this output belongs to

ToolPolicy.description: Tool policy determines how tool calls are handled

ToolProgressData.description:
Data for tool.progress event.

Emitted by tools during execution to report interim status updates.
This allows long-running tools (e.g., browser operations, sandbox setup)
to stream progress feedback between tool.started and tool.completed.

display_name.description: Human-readable display name for UI rendering

tool_call_id.description: Tool call ID this progress belongs to

ToolResultContentPart.description: Tool result content part (result of tool execution)

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call this result corresponds to

display_name.description: Human-readable display name for UI rendering

tool_call_fingerprint.description: Stable fingerprint of tool name + normalized arguments.

TranscriptRepairAction.description: Action taken during transcript repair for a dangling tool call.

TranscriptRepairedData.description:
Data for transcript.repaired event (EVE-533).

Emitted once per dangling tool call when transcript repair runs before a `reason` call.
A dangling call is an assistant `tool_call` with no matching `ToolResult` in the
message history. Repair makes the transcript well-formed so the next LLM call succeeds.

action.description: Action taken: `replay` (settled result reused) or `synthesize` (interrupted placeholder added).

tool_call_id.description: The tool call ID that was repaired.

oneOf.description: Token usage before cancellation (if available)

final_answer_preview.description: Bounded preview of the final visible assistant answer.

input_content.description: Input message content (for observability, passed through from turn.started)

llm_call_count.description: Number of LLM generation calls executed during the turn.

status.description: Optional explicit completion status for consumers that summarize turns.

tool_call_count.description: Number of tool calls completed during the turn.

oneOf.description: Aggregated token usage for all LLM calls in this turn

error_disclosure.description:
Error-disclosure mode applied to `error_code`/`error_fields`
("generic" | "standard" | "detailed"). Full diagnostic detail remains
available to operators via reason.completed failure events and tracing.

error_fields.description: Structured interpolation fields for localized error rendering.

TurnSealedData.description:
Data for turn.sealed event (EVE-534).

A sealed turn was deliberately stopped to prevent waste. It is observably
distinct from `turn.completed` (success) and `turn.failed` (error). The
`reason` is the stable wire form of `everruns_core::turn::SealReason`
(`"no_progress"` or `"budget"`).

iterations.description: Iterations completed before the turn was sealed (if known).

reason.description:
Why the turn was sealed: `"no_progress"` (crash-loop with no forward
progress) or `"budget"` (work budget exhausted).

oneOf.description: Aggregated token usage before sealing, if available.
