allOf.description:
Session - instance of agentic loop execution.
A session represents a single conversation with an agent.

active_schedule_count.description:
Number of active (enabled) schedules for this session.
Populated when the session is fetched for API responses.

agent_id.description: ID of the agent working in this session (format: agent_{32-hex}). Optional.

agent_identity_id.description: Optional resident agent identity for unattended/background execution.

agent_version_id.description: Immutable agent version captured when the session was created or rebound.

blueprint_config.description:
Validated config passed by host at blueprint spawn time.
Example: `{"target_repo": "acme/everruns"}`.

blueprint_id.description:
Blueprint ID. When set, reason_activity and act_activity build RuntimeAgent
from the blueprint definition instead of from harness_id/agent_id.

capabilities.description:
Session-level capabilities (additive to agent capabilities).
Applied after agent capabilities when building RuntimeAgent.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the session was created.

oneOf.description: Effective human owner summary.

features.description:
Aggregated UI features from all active capabilities (harness + agent + session).
Computed at read time from the capability registry.
Known features: "file_system", "schedules", "secrets", "key_value",
"sql_database", "leased_resources".

finished_at.description: Timestamp when the session finished (completed or failed).

forked_from_sequence.description:
Parent event sequence the fork was taken at (the fork point). NULL unless
this session is a fork.

forked_from_session_id.description:
Session this one was forked from. NULL for sessions that were not forked.
Distinct from `parent_session_id` (subagent nesting): forking is a
user-initiated "branch from here" relationship.

harness_id.description: ID of the harness for this session (format: harness_{32-hex}).

hints.description:
Session-level client hints — arbitrary key-value pairs declared by the
client at session creation time. These are defaults for every turn;
per-message `controls.hints` override these key-by-key (shallow merge).

Examples: `{"setup_connection": true, "rich_media": true}`

id.description: Unique identifier for the session (format: session_{32-hex}).

initial_files.description:
Session-level initial files (additive to agent initial_files).
Files with matching paths override agent/harness files; new paths are appended.

is_pinned.description:
Whether this session is pinned by the current user.
Only populated when the request has an authenticated user context.

locale.description: Locale for localized agent behavior and formatting (BCP 47, e.g. `uk-UA`).

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of LLM iterations per turn for this session.

mcpServers.description: Remote MCP servers scoped to this session only.

model_id.description:
LLM model ID to use for this session (format: model_{32-hex}).
Overrides the agent's default model if set.

oneOf.description:
Network access list controlling which hosts/URLs this session can reach.
Merged with harness and agent layers (allowed: intersect, blocked: union).

organization_id.description: Organization this session belongs to (format: org_{32-hex}).

output_preview.description: Preview text from the last assistant response (truncated).
output_preview.example: Here is a Q3 plan covering the three pillars we discussed...

oneOf.description: Owning principal summary.

parallel_tool_calls.description:
Request-level parallel tool calling preference (EVE-598).

`None` (default) preserves provider defaults. `Some(true)` signals the
provider that parallel tool calls are wanted; `Some(false)` requests at
most one tool call per turn and forces serial execution. Merged across
harness/agent/session layers (overlay wins).

parent_session_id.description:
Parent session that spawned this subagent. NULL for top-level sessions.
Used as the nesting guard in spawn_subagent.

preview.description: Preview text from the first user message (truncated).
preview.example: Help me draft the Q3 marketing plan

resolved_owner_user_id.description: Denormalized effective human owner of the owning principal lineage.

started_at.description: Timestamp when the session started executing.

status.description: Current execution status of the session.

system_prompt.description:
Session-level system prompt override.
Prepended to the agent's system prompt when building RuntimeAgent.

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering sessions.

tools.description: Client-side tools for this session (additive to agent tools).

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the session was last updated.

oneOf.description: Cumulative token usage for all LLM calls in this session.

workspace_id.description:
Workspace this session is attached to (format: wsp_{32-hex}). Owns the
session's virtual filesystem. For the default 1:1 case this mirrors the
session id, but clients should read it here rather than deriving it.

allowed_actions.description:
State-aware hypermedia actions the caller can take on this resource
next (e.g. `cancel`, `events`, `update`). Omitted from the wire
shape when empty so resources that haven't opted into the
convention don't grow their payloads.

self_url.description: Full API endpoint URL for this resource.

ui_link.description: Alias for `view_url`, used by command and MCP outputs.

view_url.description: Full UI URL for viewing this resource.

WithUrls_Session.description:
Wrapper that adds API and UI links to a serialized resource.

Uses `self_url` (not `url`) for the API link to avoid collision with
resources that already have a `url` field (e.g. McpServer). The
`allowed_actions` array carries state-aware hypermedia links — empty
(and omitted from the wire shape) until the underlying resource opts
into the convention by overriding `ResourceUrlable::allowed_actions`.

allOf.description: Skill entity (API response type)

allowed_tools.description: Comma-separated list of tool patterns this skill may invoke. `None` means inherit from the harness.

archived_at.description: Timestamp when this skill was archived, if any (RFC 3339). Archived skills are hidden from default list views.

compatibility.description: Compatibility marker describing host-runtime requirements declared by the skill (e.g. min platform version). Informational.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this skill was created (RFC 3339).

deleted_at.description: Timestamp when this skill was hard-deleted, if any (RFC 3339).

description.description: Short, agent- and user-readable summary of what the skill does and when to use it.
description.example: Extract text and tables from PDF files.

disable_model_invocation.description: When `true`, the LLM is prevented from auto-invoking this skill; only the user can trigger it explicitly.

license.description: License string as declared by the skill author (e.g. `MIT`, `Apache-2.0`). Informational; not enforced.

metadata.description: Free-form metadata declared by the skill author.

name.description: Stable kebab-case slug used to invoke the skill (e.g. `/pdf-processing` in chat). Safe to render in user-facing messages.

source_type.description: How the skill content is sourced (filesystem, URL, embedded). Determines reload semantics.

status.description: Current lifecycle status (`active`, `archived`, `deleted`).

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this skill was last updated (RFC 3339).

user_invocable.description: Whether this skill appears as a `/`-prefixed slash command for end users in chat UIs.

version.description: Semver string declared by the skill author. Free-form; sorted lexicographically when comparing.

allowed_actions.description:
State-aware hypermedia actions the caller can take on this resource
next (e.g. `cancel`, `events`, `update`). Omitted from the wire
shape when empty so resources that haven't opted into the
convention don't grow their payloads.

accepting_tasks.description: Whether the worker is currently accepting new task assignments. Disabled briefly during drains or backpressure.

activity_types.description: Activity types this worker accepts. Tasks with other activity types skip this worker.

avg_task_duration_ms.description: Average task duration in milliseconds across recent activity.

backpressure_reason.description: Human-readable reason the worker is rejecting tasks, when `accepting_tasks` is `false`.

current_load.description: Number of tasks currently executing on this worker.

hostname.description: Hostname / pod name the worker is running on. Operator hint; not used for routing.

last_heartbeat_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat from this worker (RFC 3339).

max_concurrency.description: Maximum number of tasks the worker will run concurrently.

metadata.description: Free-form worker-reported metadata (deployment, capabilities flag set, etc.).

started_at.description: Timestamp when this worker started accepting tasks (RFC 3339).

status.description: Current lifecycle status (`running`, `draining`, `stopped`, etc.).

tasks_completed.description: Total tasks this worker has completed successfully.

tasks_failed.description: Total tasks this worker has failed (including retries that were ultimately abandoned).

version.description: Build version of the worker binary.

worker_group.description: Logical group this worker belongs to (used for routing). `None` for ungrouped workers.

data.description: Page of items returned by this query.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query, across all pages.

draining.description: Workers in `draining` state, finishing in-flight tasks but not accepting new ones.

stopped.description: Workers in `stopped` state, neither running nor draining.

total_load.description: Total tasks currently in flight across all workers.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was created (RFC 3339).

data.description: Page of items returned by this query.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query, across all pages.

completed_at.description: Timestamp when this resource completed, if any (RFC 3339).

created_at.description: Timestamp when this resource was created (RFC 3339).

error.description: Human-readable error message, populated when this resource is in a failed state.

input.description: Initial input the workflow was started with. Shape varies by `workflow_type`.

result.description: Terminal result, populated when the workflow has completed successfully.

started_at.description: Timestamp when this resource started, if any (RFC 3339).

status.description: Current lifecycle status.

workflow_type.description: Workflow type identifier registered with the durable executor.

data.description: Page of items returned by this query.

total.description: Total number of items matching the query, across all pages.
